signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.21 — Iran strikes on the UAE drive WTI oil +2.77% to $104.8, fueling a risk-off bid for the USD. Despite the geopolitical shock, the +300bp Fed-BoJ carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75%) sustains the pair's floor while VIX remains at 18.3 (normal volatility). JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains a crowded carry trade; however, safe-haven JPY demand is currently offset by energy-import inflation concerns. Intervention risk remains the primary ceiling as price tests recent highs.

signals.title: AUD/USD — RBA Decision + Positioning + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7169 — The RBA is expected to hike rates to 4.35% today (May 5), potentially widening the current -35bp carry deficit vs the Fed (RBA 4.10% vs Fed 3.75%). COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows institutional positioning is heavily leaned into the hawkish outcome, while retail sentiment is 82% short. This extreme institutional vs retail divergence suggests a 'buy the rumor, sell the fact' risk if the RBA delivers only a standard 25bp hike without hawkish forward guidance.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + War Risk
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5874 — COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) shows speculators are heavily fading the RBNZ's hawkish bias (OIS pricing a hike for 27 May). Middle East tensions are weighing on the pro-cyclical NZD, but the crowded spec short position creates significant short-squeeze risk if war fears stabilize or the RBNZ reaffirms its tightening path. The +150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%) provides the structural ceiling.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Hawkishness vs Correlation Break
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1695 — ECB's Nagel signals a June hike is warranted, yet the pair remains under pressure from Middle East-driven USD demand. EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation has shifted to +0.724 (z=+1.89σ), indicating the pair is decoupling from traditional equity risk-on flows and is instead trading as a proxy for regional energy/geopolitical risk. The +175bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0%) structurally limits upside despite hawkish ECB rhetoric.
